'''Paul Smith''' (born 24 December 1969) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played for the [[Penrith Panthers]] and [[Western Suburbs Magpies]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Dollin |first=Shawn |title=Paul Smith - Playing Career - RLP |url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/paul-smith/summary.html |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20250329162206/https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/paul-smith/summary.html |archive-date=2025-03-29 |access-date=2025-12-04 |website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org |language=en}}</ref>

Smith, a product of Ashcroft High School in Sydney, played his football as a winger.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dansnrlcollectables.com/contents/en-us/p32361_Heroes-of-Yesterday,-Interview-36-%E2%80%93-5-July-2018-%E2%80%93-Paul-Smith.html|title=Heroes of Yesterday, Interview 36|date=5 July 2018|publisher=dansnrlcollectables.com|accessdate=12 October 2018}}</ref>

Debuting in 1987, Smith spent seven seasons in the Penrith first-grade side. He scored a try in Penrith's four-point grand final loss to the [[Canberra Raiders]] in 1990, then featured in the club's 1991 premiership team, this time overcoming Canberra.<ref>{{cite news |url=http://nla.gov.au/nla.news-article122386001 |title=Simmons's 'fairy-tale' exit |newspaper=[[The Canberra Times]]|date=23 September 1991 |accessdate=13 October 2018 |page=38}}
</ref> In 1991 he was a City representative in the annual [[City vs Country Origin]] match.

From 1994 to 1997 he played for Western Suburbs. He made an immediate impact in the 1994 season when his 18 tries equalled the club record, set by [[Alan Ridley]] in 1932.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.westsmagpies.com.au/the-archives/club-records/|title=Wests Archives - Club Records|publisher=westsmagpies.com.au|accessdate=12 October 2018}}</ref> His tally was equal fourth in the league that season.
